seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: generalize the input from plain co-channel conflict counting to a weighted minimum-interference frequency-assignment (MI-FAP) with per-pair required channel separations and interference weights over a geometric harbor-radio interference graph, and keep the min-conflicts objective under a fixed channel budget so the optimum is NP-hard and saturation-greedy vs min-conflicts local search vs restart heuristics diverge.
title: Gantry Chatter: Harbor Radio Channel Assignment
strategies: trivial baseline: put every radio on channel 1 (pays the full weighted separation on every pair)
saturation greedy: order radios by interference degree and assign each the channel minimizing added interference against already-placed neighbors (DSATUR-style)
min-conflicts local search: start from the greedy assignment then repeatedly move each radio to the channel minimizing its local interference until convergence
randomized restart / annealing: perturb assignments and keep the lowest-interference labeling seen across restarts
